question: If actual-LI route prepacking is cheap, why is sparse compressed dKV not dense-like yet?
short_answer: Prepacking solves the metadata problem, not the output-tile ownership and accumulator-body problem. Dense attention is fast because a GEMM-like schedule owns an output tile and reduces contributions inside that tile. The current sparse producer is query-major and fast for contribution math, but each contribution still targets an indirect compressed group. Naive attempts to make compressed groups own the output tile either lose query-major MFMA reuse, introduce per-contribution LDS/table traffic, or write/read compact partial slabs. The next viable kernel must graft grouped or segmented accumulation onto the fast query-major producer before global writes.

current_baseline.shape: DeepSeek-V4 actual LI hard layer-2 route, S=4096, compressed_ratio=4, compressed_groups=1024, topk_groups_per_token=512, heads=64, d=512

mechanism_split_ms.read: The expensive part is not the dot contribution itself; it is the many-to-one indirect accumulation/writeback.

prepack_result.read: The route can be sorted or segmented cheaply enough. Route metadata is no longer the suspected wall.

why_dense_attention_is_different.dense_schedule_property: A CTA/wave/GEMM tile owns an output tile such as dV[n,d] or dK[n,d] and reduces over the query dimension before a final write.
why_dense_attention_is_different.sparse_backward_math: dKV[g,d] = sum_{t,h,j: top_index[t,j]=g} grad_logits[t,h,j] * q[t,h,d] * scale + probs[t,h,j] * dO[t,h,d]
why_dense_attention_is_different.sparse_schedule_problem: The fast loop is naturally query-major over (token, head, selected slot), while the output is compressed-group-major over g. Without an in-tile segmented reduction, every selected slot becomes an indirect atomic add into dKV[g,d].

closed_branches.why_rejected: It exposes a clean Scatter stage but forces an HBM round trip for partials, then still performs global atomic accumulation.

It preserves the fast producer but still does one partial update per valid selected contribution and then reads the compact slab back for index_add. It is useful contract evidence, not a speedup over the 8.007 ms sparse-atomic gate.
It gets 23-47x modeled global-write reduction, but runtime stays flat and slow because the body is dominated by per-contribution LDS/table work and active-slot initialization/emission.
Switching final global atomics to compact partial rows does not fix the producer/local accumulation body.
K16-local duplicate coalescing is too weak at S4096 and preserves almost all global atomics while adding leader-scan overhead.
Persistent/local grouped accumulation loses the fast producer shape and pays too much table/schedule overhead.
It realizes the 23.81x write reduction, but recomputes H64 contribution math scalar inside group segments. Segment metadata is good; segment-owned scalar production is not.
Owning the producer by token/group panel is fine at S128/S512 but collapses at S4096 even without dKV writes. It loses query-major contribution efficiency.
A small top-N table does not capture enough groups for layer-2 actual LI. Useful reduction requires hot-768 to hot-1024, which collapses back into the wide local-table problem.

promoted_next_kernel.name: query_major_segmented_mfma_dkv_producer_v1
contract: Consume actual LI route prepacked by seq tile and compressed-group segment.
Keep the fast query-major contribution loop and MFMA/Triton-style dot schedule for grad_logits^T q and probs^T dO.
Within the producer tile, group repeated compressed-group destinations with lane/warp/block segmented reduction in registers or narrowly scoped shared memory.
Emit one compact partial row per (compressed_group, D16 block, producer tile) instead of one scalar atomic per (token, head, selected_slot, d).
Use the measured cheap rocPRIM-style reduce_by_key or equivalent final reducer for the remaining compact partial rows.
do_not_retry: per-contribution LDS atomic or per-contribution compact partial stores
global partial slab round trip as the main optimization

decision: Prepack is enough to justify the next implementation, but it does not by itself make the sparse path dense-like. The missing piece is an in-producer segmented accumulator that preserves query-major MFMA efficiency while reducing compressed-group writes before global memory.
